Chart of the Week: The odds of El Niño are heating up

Caption: The probability of El Niño is projected to rise sharply to nearly 83% by late 2026, heightening the risk of droughts and agricultural disruption across Latin America (LatAm). A weather-driven supply shock from LatAm could intensify global food inflation, adding to the existing commodity and supply-chain pressures stemming from the West Asia crisis.

items.description_html:
BREAKING: President Trump provides an update on his 48-hour visit to China for the US-China summit with 15+ US public company CEOs.

Details include:

1. Tariffs were NOT discussed with China's President Xi, Trump says they were "not brought up"

2. Trump says he made "no commitment either way" on Taiwan during talks with

3. Trump says he could meet with China's President Xi four times in 2026

4. Trump says China has not purchased Nvidia’s H200 chips, despite US approval for sales

5. Trump says he and President Xi discussed the possibility of easing US sanctions on Chinese companies buying Iranian oil

We expect further details shortly.

items.title: SJVN delivered strong revenue growth of 22% FY YoY driven by tariff order recognition and capacity additions, with EBITDA margin expanding 714 bps to ...
items.description_html:
SJVN delivered strong revenue growth of 22% FY YoY driven by tariff order recognition and capacity additions, with EBITDA margin expanding 714 bps to 80.32%.

However, execution concerns remain as capex significantly missed the target (₹945 Cr vs ₹7,500 Cr), and PAT grew only 4% despite robust revenue due to elevated finance costs and depreciation. The FY27 capex guidance of ₹8,000 Cr requires 8.5x acceleration, raising questions about delivery capability.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 109% YoY in Q4 and 22% FY YoY to ₹3,545 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 714 bps YoY to 80.32%
Record generation of 13,302 MU achieved

Buxar Unit 1 (660 MW) COD achieved, Unit 2 expected January 2026

Dividend increased to ₹1.50/share from ₹1.46 in FY25

Net worth grew 3% YoY to ₹14,708 Cr

Risk Factors

Consolidated Q4 loss of ₹118 Cr despite 197% revenue growth

Capex significantly below target: ₹945 Cr vs ₹7,500 Cr (87.4% shortfall)

Finance costs up 25% YoY, depreciation up 39% YoY
Subsidiary PAT drag of ₹366 Cr

Current ratio deteriorated from 1.82 to 0.64

HP projects at risk with ₹3,933 Cr exposure

Net profit margin contracted 505 bps despite revenue growth

items.title: WELSPUNLIV Q4 FY26 showed sequential recovery with revenue up 7.7% QoQ and EBITDA margin expanding 310 bps to 10.8%, confirming the margin trough is b...
items.description_html:
WELSPUNLIV Q4 FY26 showed sequential recovery with revenue up 7.7% QoQ and EBITDA margin expanding 310 bps to 10.8%, confirming the margin trough is behind. Domestic business growth of 29.2% YoY partially offset export weakness from US tariffs. Strong balance sheet deleveraging (net debt down 52% to ₹775 Cr) and excellent cash conversion (CFO 5.5x PAT) provide financial flexibility.

However, full-year revenue declined 11.5% and margin remains significantly behind FY27 targets of 15-16%, requiring 590-690 bps expansion - execution risk remains elevated.

Positive Factors

Revenue recovered 7.7% QoQ in Q4 to ₹2,435 Cr from ₹2,262 Cr in Q3

EBITDA margin expanded 310 bps QoQ from 7.7% to 10.8%, confirming margin trough is behind

Domestic consumer business grew 29.2% YoY in Q4, partially offsetting export weakness

Net debt reduced 52% YoY to ₹775 Cr, Debt/Equity improved to 0.36x from 0.50x

Operating cash flow strong at ₹1,175 Cr (5.5x PAT), free cash flow ₹724 Cr

Working capital released ₹662 Cr vs drain of ₹247 Cr in FY25

Finance costs declined 35.7% YoY due to significant debt reduction

Capex of ₹472 Cr exceeded guidance of ₹300 Cr, invested in growth

Risk Factors

Full-year revenue declined 11.5% YoY to ₹9,399 Cr reflecting tariff headwinds

EBITDA margin compressed 446 bps YoY from 13.6% to 9.1%

PAT declined 66.9% YoY to ₹213 Cr from ₹644 Cr

US tariff headwinds (50% on textile imports) impacted export markets

FY27 revenue target of ₹15,000 Cr requires 58.5% growth from FY26 base - highly aggressive

Margin target of 15-16% by FY27 requires 590-690 bps expansion from current 9.1%

Flooring segment EBITDA margin compressed to 3.9% from 8.3% in FY25

items.title: GOKULAGRO delivered strong Q4 FY26 with revenue up 13.51% YoY and PAT surging 164.74% YoY, driven by operating leverage (expenses grew 12.28% vs reven...
items.description_html:
GOKULAGRO delivered strong Q4 FY26 with revenue up 13.51% YoY and PAT surging 164.74% YoY, driven by operating leverage (expenses grew 12.28% vs revenue 13.51%). EBITDA margin expanded 90 bps to 3.14%. Balance sheet strengthened significantly with Debt-Equity improving to 0.40x from 0.51x.

Management approved ₹430 Cr capacity expansion (46% increase) signaling strong demand confidence. Concerns include raw material cost pressure (cost up 60.72% YoY) and weak cash conversion (0.84x). No prior management guidance available for comparison - all growth signals are organic performance, not guidance beats.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 13.51% YoY to ₹620,019 Lakh in Q4 FY26

PAT surged 164.74% YoY to ₹12,809 Lakh in Q4 FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 90 bps YoY to 3.14%

Debt-Equity improved from 0.51x to 0.40x

Total equity grew 37.34% to ₹142,281 Lakh through retained earnings

Approved ₹430 Cr capacity expansion (46% increase) over 12-18 months

Capacity utilization improved to 82.25% from ~75%
Interest coverage improved from 2.89x to 3.88x

Risk Factors

Cost of materials consumed grew 60.72% YoY vs revenue growth of 13.51% - significant raw material
cost pressure

Working capital deterioration - inventories increased ₹44,816 Lakh

Cash conversion weak - CFO/PAT at 0.84x

Foreign exchange loss on borrowings ₹140 Lakh; FCTR provision ₹1,766 Lakh

Purchase of stock-in-trade declined 50.11% YoY indicating shift from trading to manufacturing

items.title: NCC Limited reported a challenging FY26 with revenue declining 6.2% YoY to ₹20,823 cr despite a strong order book of ₹83,004 cr, indicating executio...
items.description_html:
NCC Limited reported a challenging FY26 with revenue declining 6.2% YoY to ₹20,823 cr despite a strong order book of ₹83,004 cr, indicating execution conversion challenges. Q4 showed sequential recovery with 28% QoQ revenue growth, but working capital deterioration (CFO negative ₹458.51 cr, WC absorption ₹1,972 cr) and significant debt buildup (borrowings up 47.8% current, 464% non-current) raise cash flow concerns.

The company needs to resolve payment delays from clients, particularly JJM projects, to convert its robust order book into revenue and improve cash conversion.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue recovered 28% QoQ to ₹6,232.71 cr from ₹4,868.29 cr in Q3

Order book strong at ₹83,004 cr with ₹31,884 cr orders secured during FY26

BOT project losses reduced from ₹11.48 cr to ₹2.40 cr, showing turnaround

Q4 PAT grew 60.3% QoQ to ₹216.77 cr from ₹135.22 cr in Q3

Subsidiary PAT contribution positive at ₹98.56 cr (14.6% of consolidated PAT)

Standalone CFO healthy at ₹886.05 cr vs PAT ₹576.76 cr (1.54x conversion)

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue declined 6.2% YoY to ₹20,823 cr despite strong order book - execution conversion challenged

Working capital absorption severe at ₹1,972.11 cr; trade receivables up 12.8%, inventories up 35.1%

CFO negative at ₹458.51 cr (FY25 positive ₹741.70 cr) - cash quality deteriorated

FCF severely negative at ₹1,413.82 cr

Debt increased materially: current borrowings up 47.8%, non-current up 464%

Finance costs up 9.6% YoY vs guidance of 4-5%, reflecting higher debt levels

NHAI debarment under interim protection - regulatory risk remains

items.title: Nava Ltd reported 12.2% YoY revenue growth to ₹1,14,285 Lakh driven by Energy segment strength, but PAT declined 55% YoY due to non-cash deferred tax...
items.description_html:
Nava Ltd reported 12.2% YoY revenue growth to ₹1,14,285 Lakh driven by Energy segment strength, but PAT declined 55% YoY due to non-cash deferred tax expense of ₹16,326 Lakh from Zambian Kwacha appreciation.

Operating cash flow was strong at 2.22x PAT, and ZESCO receivables collection reached 95%. MEL Phase II (300 MW) remains on track for H2 FY27 commissioning with $400M total capex.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 12.2% YoY to ₹1,14,285 Lakh driven by Energy segment (+13% YoY)

Operating cash flow exceeded PAT by 2.22x (₹2,31,233 Lakh vs ₹1,03,852 Lakh)

ZESCO receivables reduced to ₹25,990 Lakh from ₹1,37,478 Lakh (95% collected)

Current ratio improved to 7.62x from 5.98x indicating strong liquidity

Ferro Alloys segment results grew 634.5% YoY to ₹1,455 Lakh

Free cash flow positive at ₹50,514 Lakh despite heavy capex of ₹1,80,719 Lakh

Risk Factors

PAT declined 55% YoY due to deferred tax expense of ₹16,326 Lakh from FX-related accounting adjustments

EBITDA margin contracted 500 bps YoY to 32.5% due to 79% increase in employee costs and 34.9% increase in manufacturing expenses

Mining segment results declined 26% YoY to ₹10,960 Lakh with margin compression to 22.8% from 32.5%

Finance costs rose 49.2% YoY to ₹708 Lakh reflecting higher borrowings

Debt/Equity increased to 0.21x from 0.09x due to project financing

items.title: MODIS reported strong YoY revenue (158%) and earnings growth (191%), though comparisons are distorted by the merger with Shree Modi's Navnirman Privat...
items.description_html:
MODIS reported strong YoY revenue (158%) and earnings growth (191%), though comparisons are distorted by the merger with Shree Modi's Navnirman Private Limited. The company showed concerning margin compression (496 bps) driven by Cost of Projects growing 18x faster than revenue, and negative operating cash flow of ₹233 Lakh indicating earnings are not converting to cash.

No management guidance was provided in the results, so no guidance beats or misses can be assessed. The balance sheet remains healthy with conservative leverage (0.036x D/E) and strong current ratio (2.09x).

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 158.2% YoY in Q4 and 84% YoY for FY26

PAT grew 191.1% YoY to ₹440.59 Lakh in Q4

EPS grew 192.2% YoY to ₹2.25

Equity grew 22.8% YoY to ₹15,666.09 Lakh

Current ratio improved to 2.09x indicating strong
liquidity

Debt/Equity remains conservative at 0.036x despite 68% increase in borrowings

Employee and other expenses showed strong operating leverage declining 86% and 88% YoY respectively

Risk Factors

Cost of Projects grew 2,944.6% YoY vs 158% revenue growth - massive margin drag

EBITDA margin compressed 496 bps YoY from 19.6% to 14.7%

Finance costs surged 239% YoY reflecting increased borrowings

Operating cash flow negative at ₹233.38 Lakh - earnings not converting to cash

Other income at 47.3% of PBT includes ₹57.19 Lakh one-time gains

Trade receivables grew 570% YoY to ₹879.63 Lakh

Inventory increased 41.4% YoY to ₹14,642.21 Lakh

items.description_html:
RT CSEINDIA
Why India’s summer isn’t as hot as expected?

India was expected to face an intense heatwave summer in 2026. Instead, many regions are seeing cooler temperatures, thunderstorms, hailstorms, and excess rainfall. What caused this dramatic shift?

In this video, Down To Earth's @bluewrit explains the multiple weather systems shaping India’s unusual summer: from western disturbances and cyclonic circulations to the possible influence of El Niño and the approaching monsoon.

items.title: Rane Holdings delivered strong FY26 revenue growth of 34.9% to ₹5,883 Cr with Q4 EBITDA margin improving to 9.88% (near double-digit target). The tur...
items.description_html:
Rane Holdings delivered strong FY26 revenue growth of 34.9% to ₹5,883 Cr with Q4 EBITDA margin improving to 9.88% (near double-digit target). The turnaround in JV/associate profits (from ₹69.3 Cr loss to ₹26 Cr profit) and significant debt reduction (debt/equity from 0.74x to 0.62x) demonstrate operational improvement.

Normalized PAT grew 41.6% YoY, but working capital pressure and negative FCF of ₹360 Cr are concerns. Q4 margin trajectory suggests management is on track to achieve 10%+ EBITDA target in FY27.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 34.9% YoY to ₹5,883 Cr in FY26

Q4 EBITDA margin improved to 9.88% from 7.42% YoY (+246 bps)

PAT to owners swung to profit of ₹74.2 Cr in Q4 from loss in Q3

Share of JV profit swung from ₹69.3 Cr loss to ₹26 Cr profit QoQ

Debt reduced by ₹11,285 Lakh, debt/equity improved from 0.74x to 0.62x

Normalized PAT grew 41.6% YoY to ₹121 Cr

Operating leverage visible with expenses growing 250 bps slower than revenue

Risk Factors

FY26 EBITDA margin at 7.45% missed double-digit target (10%+) by 255 bps

Working capital deterioration: receivables grew 15.8% and inventories 16.4% vs revenue growth of 34.9%

CFO declined 45.5% YoY to ₹2,796 Cr from ₹5,130 Cr

Free cash flow turned negative at ₹360 Cr (negative FCF of ₹3,603 Lakh)

Warranty provision of ₹8,701 Lakh in ZLRAI for
quality/safety issues requiring potential recalls

NCI contribution to PAT increased to 28.4% from 6.2%, reducing minority shareholders' stake

items.title: SOLARINDS delivered exceptional FY26 results with revenue growth of 25.2% YoY and PAT growth of 52.1% YoY. EBITDA margin expanded by 393 bps to 27.93%...
items.description_html:
SOLARINDS delivered exceptional FY26 results with revenue growth of 25.2% YoY and PAT growth of 52.1% YoY. EBITDA margin expanded by 393 bps to 27.93%, exceeding management's 27-28% guidance range. The defense segment emerged as a major growth driver with 72% YoY growth and ₹18,000 Cr order book providing multi-year visibility.

Balance sheet remains robust with Debt/Equity at 0.03 and interest coverage at 72x. FY27 outlook is strong with management targeting ₹13,000-14,000 Cr revenue and defense execution ramping up.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 25.2% YoY to ₹5,578.86 Cr in FY26

PAT grew 52.1% YoY to ₹1,221.70 Cr in FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 393 bps YoY to 27.93%

Defense segment grew 72% YoY in Q3 with ₹18,000 Cr
order book

Debt Equity ratio improved to 0.03 from 0.06

Interest coverage ratio improved to 72.19x from 35.11x

Net worth grew 43.2% YoY to ₹6,548.90 Cr

CRISIL outlook upgraded to Positive

Risk Factors

Free cash flow negative at ₹1,118.25 Cr due to heavy capex

Working capital deteriorated with receivables up ₹633.73 Cr and inventories up ₹701.19 Cr

Consolidated revenue 23.9% lower than standalone due to inter-company eliminations

Turkey subsidiaries IndAS 29 hyperinflation charge of ₹94.53 Cr

items.title: Thangamayl delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue up 105.7% YoY and PAT up 354.3% YoY, significantly beating management's own guidance on ...
items.description_html:
Thangamayl delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with revenue up 105.7% YoY and PAT up 354.3% YoY, significantly beating management's own guidance on customer base (45+ vs 40 Lakh), SSS growth (38% vs 10% target), and gross margin (+215 bps vs 100 bps target).

Operating leverage of 770 bps drove margin expansion of 337 bps YoY, while the shift to higher-margin non-gold products (141% YoY growth) and strong same-store growth demonstrate sustainable competitive advantages. Near-term risk from import duty hike is partially mitigated by the exchange model (50-60% of sales).

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 105.7% YoY in Q4 to ₹2,838 Cr, full year FY26 grew 73.2% to ₹8,499 Cr

PAT grew 354.3% YoY in Q4 to ₹142.7 Cr, full year FY26
grew 196.2% to ₹351.7 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 337 bps YoY to 7.51% in Q4

Customer base exceeded target by 12.5%: 45+ Lakhs vs 40 Lakh target

Same-store sales growth of 38.18% far exceeded 10% target

Non-gold products grew 141% YoY, mix shifting to higher margin items

Operating leverage delivered 770 bps positive delta - costs grew slower than revenue

Store network expanded to 66 stores, up from 60

Risk Factors

Import duty on gold/silver increased from 6% to 15% effective 13/05/2026 - near-term demand uncertainty

Tax litigation exposure of ₹70.17 Cr (CBDT Review stayed collection but awaiting appellate order)

Store expansion at 6 net new stores vs 8+ target - slightly below plan

Current ratio declined from 1.72x to 1.54x - liquidity modest deterioration

items.title: LAST DAY: On Friday, Jerome Powell’s term as chair of the US Federal Reserve Board of Governors comes to end after months of tension between White Ho...
items.description_html:
LAST DAY: On Friday, Jerome Powell’s term as chair of the US Federal Reserve Board of Governors comes to end after months of tension between White House and @federalreserve , as US President Donald Trump pushes for more aggressive interest rate cuts.
He will be succeeded by Trump appointee Kevin Warsh, who served on central bank’s board of governors from 2006 to 2011. Powell will continue on the board as a governor after stepping down as chair.

items.title: SOLARA reported a Q4 turnaround with PAT turning positive at ₹9.60 Cr after three consecutive quarterly losses, and strong revenue growth of 41.9% Yo...
items.description_html:
SOLARA reported a Q4 turnaround with PAT turning positive at ₹9.60 Cr after three consecutive quarterly losses, and strong revenue growth of 41.9% YoY. However, FY26 performance significantly missed management guidance across all key metrics - revenue growth of 6.6% vs ~10% target, EBITDA of ₹186.75 Cr vs ₹240-250 Cr target, and margin compression of 250 bps vs improvement target.

The company faces ongoing liquidity stress with negative working capital and high leverage (net debt/EBITDA 3.53x). CRAMS expansion represents a potential growth catalyst but remains 3-4 years away. Debt reduction and rights issue provide some balance sheet support, but execution on margin recovery and deleveraging remains critical.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 PAT turned positive at ₹9.60 Cr after three quarters of losses

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 41.9% YoY to ₹387.29 Cr, highest quarterly revenue in FY26

Q4 FY26 EBITDA margin expanded 420 bps QoQ to 14.9%

Gross debt reduced by ₹111.52 Cr (14.4%) to ₹664.54 Cr

Equity increased 14.5% YoY to ₹1,255.55 Cr from rights issue

Free cash flow remained positive at ₹100.09 Cr

Operating cash flow at ₹152.91 Cr despite 28.2% YoY decline

CRAMS business targeting 4-5x growth to ₹400-500 Cr in 3-4 years

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue growth of 6.6% missed management guidance of ~10% by 340 bps

FY26 EBITDA of ₹186.75 Cr missed guidance of ₹240-250 Cr by ₹53-63 Cr

FY26 EBITDA margin compressed 250 bps YoY vs target of 200-300 bps improvement

Trade receivables grew 34.3% YoY (₹111.32 Cr increase) indicating working capital stress

Net debt-to-EBITDA ratio at 3.53x, far from target of 1.5x by Q1 FY27

Net current liabilities exceed current assets by ₹35.81 Cr; going concern risk explicitly flagged

Gross debt at ₹664.54 Cr vs target of ₹500 Cr by May 2026, missing by ₹165 Cr

items.title: Jain Irrigation missed all major FY26 guidance targets - revenue grew only 10.7% vs 15%+ target, EBITDA margin was 12.5% vs 13%+ target, and debt incr...
items.description_html:
Jain Irrigation missed all major FY26 guidance targets - revenue grew only 10.7% vs 15%+ target, EBITDA margin was 12.5% vs 13%+ target, and debt increased by ₹261 Cr vs targeted reduction. The company swung to a loss of ₹39.99 Cr due to ₹105.86 Cr in exceptional items.

However, the Hi-tech Agri segment showed strong 20.5% growth and the beverage project provides a potential FY27 catalyst. Management's credibility is damaged by the guidance misses and FY27 targets of 18% revenue growth and 14-14.5% EBITDA margin appear ambitious.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 10.7% YoY to ₹6,399.52 Cr despite guidance miss

Hi-tech Agri Input Products segment delivered strong 20.5% revenue growth and 23.7% EBIT growth

Q4 EBITDA margin recovered to 13.0% from Q3's 10.4% trough

Operating cash flow strong at ₹618.77 Cr with positive free cash flow of ₹265.19 Cr

Normalized PAT of ₹35.55 Cr shows 38.4% YoY growth excluding one-time items

Agro Processing segment EBIT grew 34.1% YoY
showing turnaround in that division

Q4 revenue showed sequential recovery with 14.2% QoQ growth

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue of 10.7% missed 15%+ guidance target by 430 bps

Company swung to loss of ₹39.99 Cr from profit of ₹25.69 Cr in FY25

Debt increased by ₹261 Cr vs target reduction of >₹400 Cr - opposite direction

Plastic Division EBIT declined 27.3% YoY with margin at 7.2% vs 12-14% target

Current ratio declined to 1.16x from 1.31x indicating liquidity pressure

Exceptional items of ₹105.86 Cr including MAT transition charge impacted results

Credit rating outlook negative with CRISIL revising to Negative

items.title: Rapido’s overall round is $730 million, of which $240 million is primary capital. This implies a massive secondary sale of nearly $500 million by exi...
items.description_html:
Rapido’s overall round is $730 million, of which $240 million is primary capital.

This implies a massive secondary sale of nearly $500 million by existing investors - one of the largest private market exits over the last 18 months.

Swiggy sold 12% stake in Rapido for over $250 million.

It is likely that Rapido's early investors are also exiting, fully or partially, given that Rapido is now an 11-year-old company.


The Arc: 🚨 Rapido has raised $240 mn in funding at $3 bn valuation - a significant jump from $1.1 bn valuation in Sep 2024.

Venture firm Accel has joined as the new investor. The round was led by existing backer Prosus.

Rapido is taking on Uber in India's ride-hailing market.

items.title: DEVYANI reported strong revenue growth of 18.5% YoY to ₹14,369 million in Q4 FY26 with positive SSSG recovery in KFC India from January 2026 and Biry...
items.description_html:
DEVYANI reported strong revenue growth of 18.5% YoY to ₹14,369 million in Q4 FY26 with positive SSSG recovery in KFC India from January 2026 and Biryani by Kilo achieving breakeven 3 months early. However, the company reported a loss of ₹98 million with EBITDA margin compression of 267 bps YoY to 13.7%, reflecting elevated employee costs and continued investment in new store expansion.

Strong FCF generation of ₹4,602 million provides liquidity buffer. The Sapphire Foods merger pending and Sky Gate integration remain key catalysts for FY27 profitability turnaround.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 18.5% YoY to ₹14,369 million, accelerating from Q3's 11.3% growth

Biryani by Kilo achieved breakeven in December 2025, 3 months ahead of March 2026 target

KFC India SSSG turned positive in January 2026

Free cash flow strong at ₹4,602 million despite operating loss

Operating cash flow to EBITDA conversion healthy at 1.08x

Sky Gate became wholly-owned subsidiary in March 2026 after acquiring remaining stake

Trade payables increased ₹1,442 million improving vendor funding

Risk Factors

Reported loss of ₹98 million in Q4 FY26 and full-year loss of ₹425 million

EBITDA margin compressed 267 bps YoY to 13.7%

Employee costs grew 22.6% YoY, faster than revenue (18.5%), pressuring margins by 50 bps

Total borrowings increased 46% YoY to ₹13,604 million

Current ratio at 0.51x indicates working capital pressure

Pizza Hut turnaround still in progress with rationalization of loss-making stores ongoing

items.title: Hester Biosciences reported strong Q4 with 22.2% revenue growth and 973.7% PAT growth driven by Poultry Healthcare margin expansion (+1,410 bps). Anim...
items.description_html:
Hester Biosciences reported strong Q4 with 22.2% revenue growth and 973.7% PAT growth driven by Poultry Healthcare margin expansion (+1,410 bps). Animal Healthcare returned to growth (+3.1% YoY) after H1 declines.

New catalysts including H9N2 vaccine licenses and BSL-3 facility capitalization position well for FY27. Balance sheet strengthened with debt/equity improving to 0.19x. However, no explicit management guidance was provided in the source to benchmark against for surprise assessment.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue grew 22.2% YoY to ₹1,001.10 Mn with 29.3% QoQ acceleration

PAT grew 973.7% YoY in Q4 to ₹165.46 Mn, FY26 PAT up 99.4% to ₹574.84 Mn

EBITDA margin expanded 1,410 bps YoY from 25.0% to 39.1% in Q4

Poultry Healthcare margin improved to 35.7% from 21.6% (+1,410 bps)

Animal Healthcare returned to growth with +3.1% YoY in Q4 after H1 decline

Debt/Equity improved from 0.31x to 0.19x, non-current borrowings down 57.7%

OCF/PAT conversion strong at 97.7% standalone, 111.6% consolidated

H9N2 Avian Influenza vaccine received marketing and manufacturing licenses

Risk Factors

Animal Healthcare FY26 revenue declined 11.8% YoY to ₹1,266.28 Mn

Q4 consolidated PAT drag of ₹98.38 Mn vs standalone due to forex losses in Africa

items.title: RT Pahlé India Foundation: The Supreme Court's verdict in Harish Rana v. Union of India was a landmark moment — confirming that the right to die wit...
items.description_html:
RT Pahlé India Foundation
The Supreme Court's verdict in Harish Rana v. Union of India was a landmark moment — confirming that the right to die with dignity isn't just constitutional aspiration but an operational framework.

But as Dr @urvashi01 and Ankeetaa Mahesshwari from @PahleIndia argues in @down2earthindia, the case settles one question only to sharpen another: when accessing this right still requires years of litigation, medical boards, and a path to the Supreme Court itself, is it a right available to everyone — or only to those who can afford to fight for it?

A thoughtful read on what India's end-of-life jurisprudence has achieved, and what Parliament still needs to do.

🔗Read the full piece here-  https://www.downtoearth.org.in/health/harish-rana-makes-plain-that-indian-law-takes-dying-with-dignity-seriously-the-question-now-is-whether-it-takes-it-seriously-for-everyone

#RightToDieWithDignity #HarishRana #PassiveEuthanasia #Article21 #HealthPolicy #IndianLaw


Down To Earth: Comment

Harish Rana makes plain that Indian law takes dying with dignity seriously. The question now is whether it takes it seriously for everyone, write
Urvashi Prasad and Ankeetaa Mahesshwari

https://www.downtoearth.org.in/health/harish-rana-makes-plain-that-indian-law-takes-dying-with-dignity-seriously-the-question-now-is-whether-it-takes-it-seriously-for-everyone

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: He skipped placements at IIT Madras for 2 consecutive days. On the third day, he took a flight back to Manga...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
He skipped placements at IIT Madras for 2 consecutive days. On the third day, he took a flight back to Mangalore just to avoid being forced by his family to attend placements. His desire to build an aircraft company was so intense that it made him act irrationally in front of his family.

He then started building his aircraft company from Bengaluru. When he had barely Rs 20,000 in hand, he still hired an intern, promising a stipend of Rs 7,000. On one of the days when he could not make it back to his PG on time, he spent the night at the Majestic bus stand. All of this at the age of 23!

That is the story of @shreepoorna365, founder of Arctus Aerospace, a company building unmanned aerial vehicles that can fly up to 45,000 feet and capture imagery for a wide range of use cases.

Hear his full story on the @mundhebanni podcast, with English subtitles for those who do not follow Kannada. 👇
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qiXXbrt1J7M&t=1905s

items.title: Access to safe, usable toilet facilities is a critical component of public health and safety. India has long struggled with very low levels of sanitat...
items.description_html:
Access to safe, usable toilet facilities is a critical component of public health and safety.

India has long struggled with very low levels of sanitation, significantly worse than many comparable countries. In 2000, only 14% of India’s population had access to at least a basic sanitation facility, placing it behind much poorer countries in Sub-Saharan Africa, such as Somalia. When people do not have access to a safe and usable toilet, they are forced to defecate in the open.

However, over the next two decades, India made consistent progress in improving access to sanitation. By 2022, India had surpassed countries that were at a similar level in 2000, or were ahead at the time.

Basic sanitation facilities are defined as improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households. These include flush/pour-flush to piped sewer systems, septic tanks or pit latrines; ventilated improved pit latrines, composting toilets or pit latrines with slabs. This categorisation is based on WHO and UNICEF's definition.

What kind of toilet facilities do Indian households have access to? Read @nileena_suresh's piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/sanitation/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Toilets #Sanitation #WASH #India #DataForIndia

items.title: RT CHINA MFA Spokesperson 中国外交部发言人: Q: It is reported that during President Trump’s visit to China, the two presidents exchanged views on maj...
items.description_html:
RT CHINA MFA Spokesperson 中国外交部发言人
Q: It is reported that during President Trump’s visit to China, the two presidents exchanged views on major international and regional issues, including the situation in the Middle East. Did the two sides talk about Iran? What is China’s view on the current Iran situation?

A: China’s position on the Iran situation is very clear. The conflict has inflicted severe losses on the people in Iran and other regional countries. With the spillover still expanding, the conflict has put a heavy strain on global economic growth, supply chains, international trade order and the stability of global energy supply, which hurts the common interests of the international community. There is no point in continuing this conflict which should not have happened in the first place. To find an early way to resolve the situation is in the interest of not only the U.S. and Iran, but also regional countries and the rest of the world.

The recent U.S.-Iran ceasefire and effort to explore resolving issues through negotiation is welcomed by regional countries and the international community. China always believes that dialogue and negotiation is the right way forward, and the use of force is a dead end. Now that the door of dialogue has been opened, it should not be shut again. It is important to steady the momentum in easing the situation, keep to the direction of political settlement, engage in dialogue and consultation, and reach a settlement on the Iranian nuclear issue and other issues that accommodates the concerns of all parties. It is important to reopen the shipping lanes as soon as possible to respond to the call of the international community and jointly keep the global supply chains stable and unimpeded. It is important to reach a comprehensive and lasting ceasefire as soon as possible, enable peace and stability to return to the Middle East and Gulf region at an early date, and lay the foundation for building a sustainable security architecture for the region.

Since the conflict began, China has been working for an end to the conflict and peace. President Xi Jinping put forward the four propositions on safeguarding and promoting peace and stability in the Middle East. China and Pakistan issued the five-point initiative for restoring peace and stability in the Gulf and Middle East region. China will continue to act in line with President Xi Jinping’s four propositions, and work with the international community to provide stronger support for peace talks and play a constructive role in achieving lasting peace in the Middle East.

Dolat Capital estimates suggest the hike reduces daily losses by ~₹1.41bn, though a further ~₹11/ltr increase is still needed to fully eliminate losses.

Key positives for OMCs:
- Signals possible return to fuel price deregulation
- Helps reduce short-term working capital borrowing
- Eases investor concerns around losses, book value & net worth

Source: Dolat Capital

@CNBCTV18Live

items.title: US oil reserves are falling at a record pace: The Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) dropped -8.6 million barrels last week, the largest weekly drawdow...
items.description_html:
US oil reserves are falling at a record pace:

The Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) dropped -8.6 million barrels last week, the largest weekly drawdown on record, per Zerohedge.

This marks the 7th consecutive weekly decline, the longest streak since 2023.

Over this period, US oil reserves in the SPR have dropped -31 million barrels, to 384 million, the lowest since October 2024.

This wipes out nearly half of all the oil added to the SPR between July 2023 and March 2026.

Meanwhile, US crude oil exports jumped +742,000 barrels last week, to 5.5 million barrels per day, the 2nd-highest in at least 3 years, after a record 6.5 million barrels per day 2 weeks prior.

The world is scrambling for American oil.

items.title: Retail investors are piling into equities at a historic pace: Cumulative retail equity inflows year-to-date are tracking above every comparable period...
items.description_html:
Retail investors are piling into equities at a historic pace:

Cumulative retail equity inflows year-to-date are tracking above every comparable period over the last 7 years, except 2021.

After a brief pause in March, retail purchases surged in April to a level seen only 13% of the time in monthly data since 2019.

In the week ending May 1st, retail purchases ranked in the top 2% of all weekly inflows recorded since 2019.

At this pace, cumulative purchases by individual investors will exceed the 2021 record by as early as July.

Furthermore, the average daily volume of options traded by retail investors is up to 1.57x of January 2024 levels, the highest since the October 2025 record.

Risk appetite among retail investors is accelerating.

items.title: BREAKING: The 3x leveraged long semiconductor ETF, $SOXL, attracted a record +$1.03 billion in inflows on Tuesday. At the same time, the 3x leveraged ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The 3x leveraged long semiconductor ETF, $SOXL, attracted a record +$1.03 billion in inflows on Tuesday.

At the same time, the 3x leveraged short semiconductor ETF, $SOXS, posted -$230 million in outflows, the largest daily outflow since late March.

Furthermore, the 3x leveraged long Nasdaq 100 ETF, $TQQQ, saw +$161 million in inflows, the highest daily intake since March 31.

In other words, $SOXL inflows were more than 6 times larger than $TQQQ inflows, as investors concentrated their bullish bets in semiconductors.

$SOXL is now up +354% since the March 30th bottom, the largest 31-day increase since the fund launched in 2010.

This comes as the semiconductor sector, $SOX, is up +68% over the same period, the 3rd-best 31-day performance on record.

The run in semiconductor stocks is truly historic.

items.title: Silver’s outperformance of gold is gaining momentum: The gold-to-silver ratio is down to 54, the 2nd-lowest since February 2023. This ratio has falle...
items.description_html:
Silver’s outperformance of gold is gaining momentum:

The gold-to-silver ratio is down to 54, the 2nd-lowest since February 2023.

This ratio has fallen -9 points over the last 6 trading days, with silver outperforming gold for 6 consecutive sessions, the longest streak since December 2025.

Over this period, silver prices have surged +20%, to $87 per ounce, the highest since March 11th, while gold prices have increased just +3%, to $4,690 per ounce.

The ratio is now down -51 points, or -49%, since the April 2025 peak of 105 points.

By comparison, the ratio briefly touched 43 points, the lowest level in 15 years, in January 2026, after silver prices surpassed $120 per ounce for the first time in history.

Keep watching precious metals.

items.title: India is apparently considering removing tax on fiis investing in Indian bonds. This is a good thing. Why? Foreign investors pay tax on all capital ga...
items.description_html:
India is apparently considering removing tax on fiis investing in Indian bonds. This is a good thing. Why?

Foreign investors pay tax on all capital gains made in India. India is different from all major economies who follow residence based taxation meaning capital gains are taxed in the country of your residence not the country inwhich you invest. If you buy a US listed stocks and sell it at a profit , from India., the US does not tax you (india does)

For bonds it is a pain because you get taxed for a capital gain at 20% in India. The fpi may be an index fund buying a bond index in which Indian securities are, and they will underperform that index because the tax is deducted in India at source. Bonds incur capital gains when interest rates fall, and if they are sold the fpi pays. Note that an Indian institution like a mutual fund , insurance co or a pension fund pays no tax.

Also the interest is complex. When you buy a bond in the secondary market you pay the price plus the accrued interest from the bond after its last interest payment date. Apparently fpis get hit because even getting paid that accrued interest when they sell a bond has that tax, and ifntheir jurisdictions don't tax mutual funds or pension funds there is no corresponding tax in their home countries to offset what's paid in India. Sadly even if there's only a 5% tax on interest i think the tds is 20%, which sucks double big time cos you have to wait about a year to get a refund.

India should get moving with this and make capital gains and interest on listed bonds paid to fpis totally tax free on cap gains and only 5% tds on interest (or none at all)

We should also eliminate cap gains taxes on stocks for them also just like we don't tax indian institutions on such gains. This is how it is internationally.

It won't reverse fpi flows really but in the long term it will attract foreign capital and allow the rupee to internationalise.

items.title: RT Kushal Lodha: Had an amazing time at @NSEIndia meeting MD & CEO, @ashishchauhan and having a tour of the entire building. What stood out was the tr...
items.description_html:
RT Kushal Lodha
Had an amazing time at @NSEIndia meeting MD & CEO, @ashishchauhan and having a tour of the entire building.

What stood out was the trading floor, and the amazing cyber security system that NSE has in order to ensure there are no cyber scams that happen.

It was always a dream to ring the bell at NSE, although it'll be even better if it is by listing my own company haha, but at least the manifestation begins now.

What an amazing day, surrounded with the best content creators in world of finance and business, and meeting them after so long. It is always a pleasure to learn from creators in your category. I highly recommend everyone to form a group of 3-5 content creators in your similar category and of your size so you guys can brainstorm every week / fortnight about content.

Thank you so much to @PurpleBlueHouse and @PreritaC for helping us organise this and making this happen. Also, thank you so much for the wonderful dinner at the Intercontinental Hotel with the marvellous sunset view!

Looking forward to more of such opportunities in the future.

Highly grateful!

#NSE #finance #motivation #inspiration

items.title: RT ValueEquity: Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts: 1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as ...
items.description_html:
RT ValueEquity
Q4FY26 pessimism rate of change tracker, my thoughts:

1)Microfinance : Clear signs of recovery across the board as we had expected as well here , improvement in asset quality and loan growth is visible , players like Northern arc have already shown strong numbers with good visibility as well compared to last year , thing to note here is that it will not be as linear as the last cycle , as we are seeing from sector wide commentaries that there is a problem with the JLG mode of business , and Armaan one of the best players in this space has been pointing this out for a few quarters now , I think the leaders will be the ones that can keep coming up with newer and more innovative products for their asset side to keep growing well , also market will now pay a geography multiple as well , we have seen state governments get increasingly hostile with what happened in Karnataka last year , and hence geographical diversification as well as the quality newer products will be the factors to watch , Northern Arc and Armaan seem to be ahead in this space , players like northern arc are far more enabled for sustainable growth that is data driven and cross cyclical

2) Chemicals : Things are starting to take shape in this sector in the commodity chemicals side of it as we had already turned positive in Q3 and overweight 2 weeks back , there are multiple factors leading to this from the Middle eastern conflicts causing the first layer of inflationary price hikes and improvements in the spreads , added by the Chinese anti involution policy that is helping on the rationalization of the overall pricing environment for the commodities, on the Nitration front as well because of the large fire incident in March in China there will now be much tighter regulations and that may affect some smaller players again leading to some price inflation in some chemicals

Yes there have been some RM shortages also as highlighted by the companies but again the larger players are much more equipped to keep dealing with this

Amines duo's , Alkyl has commented that there is clear improvement in the spreads and they are not seeing prices cool off to the level before the conflict any time soon , on their methyl's portfolio there is some competition because after many years aarti has set up a plant here , but that does not exist in the ethyl's side , which is still largely a 2 player market in size

As of now this is more of a supply side price situation but this is helping industry wide spreads ,Alkyl , Styrenix performance and Aarti will be beneficiaries in what I like

3) MDF/Plywood /Laminates - there are clear signs of market structure improving in early stages and the larger players benefiting , Greenply has spoken about a successful 15% price hikes in MDF and 4-5% price hikes in the Plywood segment , and there is a lot of stress in the smaller players in this space because of very high RM costs leading to very tight terms of supply , quoting the management " some suppliers are blackmailing smaller players" and " many smaller players are in a hand to mouth " situation , this is clearly indicating market share gains for larger players and inflationary gains helping them post a capex cycle , they have also invested into better Plywood technology that should help them increase the quality which will compare with high quality Asian peers

Clear indications of : Market share gains + Better pricing , hence a larger pool of profits

Greenlam has just ended a large capex cycle and they should also see their TAM expand substantially and then according to channel checks some improved pricing , and hence could also see better times ahead if the demand also starts recovering on this front

4) PVC pipes - From speaking to friends and associates doing business in the Industry , there has been an absolute rollercoaster ride in the resin prices starting Feb -march , first doubling then correcting severely and then stabilizing finally , things seem to be improving here as well as we see players like supreme focusing aggressively on market share. Apollo pipes has also turned aggressive now targeting 30% growth with Sanjay Gupta , the main man from APL Apollo steel tubes business coming here the management tone has completely changed , their strategy to growth has gotten clear with Kisan , taking their realizations higher as well and increasing focus on leveraging the dealer network of APL apollo tubes into the pipes so they can engage higher volumes and focus on increasing the market share ( disc: we are invested in Apollo pipes from 273 , and it has done well quickly , no reco ) , but now the question is on how sustainable this remains considering the supply structure and the expectations of government spending coming back as a election cycle has just ended , but can say with some confidence that the worst is over for this space, focus now now on the better players ( valuation/growth adjusted)

Now the new addition which is IT

5) The IT space has seen massive disruption , I was speaking to the fund manager at one of India's largest Family offices recently and he also termed it as a "de rating " event for the sector which will still have companies surviving , but it is early to say who will gain from it yet , which I kind of agree with , but I think we must start reading and focusing on the companies that are enabling Agentic AI solutions at enterprise level and already at the forefront of delivering such enterprise deployable efficiency based solutions to their existing client pools

I see companies like Indegene , Coforge , Intellect and few others at the forefront of being agile and prepared for the next levers of growth because they are already enabling the AI integrated offerings in which billables are more outcome based and not the typical hours based

On AI code , to quote the management of Coforge

" The deflationary argument assumes code generation is the entire job. It is not. AI-generated code is cheap to build, but it is expensive to own. It is expensive to secure and it is expensive to maintain. Just like cloud migration, agentic , AI will create a massive managed services layer. And once these systems are in
production, someone has to monitor the models, retrain the agents, and ensure governance. That is a recurring, high-margin revenue stream for firms that can seize it"

So companies that can create this AI first layer that integrates with agility and can also create cost and margin benefits in the future to the enterprise AI players will take a large part of the profit pool home , which is where opportunities for us will lie and we must be prepared to take those

That's it for this tracker folks , if you found some value in this do share it with someone who may be interested , and comment if you did

@ishmohit1

disc: none of the securities mentioned above are investment advices or financial reco's, I may be biased in few of the names mentioned as this is just my opinion from public information

items.title: RT Nithin Kamath: One thing that feels under-discussed in all the conversations about attracting foreign capital into India is the Indian diaspora. Th...
items.description_html:
RT Nithin Kamath
One thing that feels under-discussed in all the conversations about attracting foreign capital into India is the Indian diaspora.

There is a large population of NRIs who are emotionally and financially interested in investing in India. But today, for many of them, the process of opening accounts, completing documentation, and actually investing in Indian markets is still far more painful than it needs to be.

Making life easier for NRIs could be one of the lowest-hanging fruits for attracting long-term capital into India.

This is something we’ve been focusing on heavily at @zerodha as well. Over the last year or so, we’ve made several changes to make investing as seamless as possible for NRIs. But there are still many frictions that exist because of regulatory and compliance requirements.

Hopefully, SEBI and the government look at this more closely and think about how to make it easier for NRIs to bring money into India and participate in Indian markets. For a country trying to attract global capital, the Indian diaspora seems like the most obvious place to start.

items.title: My latest EAC-PM working paper: "How to do Process Reform: Case Study of IEPFA" (co-authored with @AakankshaArora5). The paper lays out step-by-step h...
items.description_html:
My latest EAC-PM working paper: "How to do Process Reform: Case Study of IEPFA" (co-authored with @AakankshaArora5). The paper lays out step-by-step how process reforms were implemented at IEFPA over the last one year. As a result, the system is now able to do more approvals in a month than it was earlier doing in a year. Indeed, the backlog will be fully cleared in the next six months. This case study is part of our effort to document government re-engineering efforts so that they can be taught and replicated across the system.

https://eacpm.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Case-study-on-IEPFA.pdf

items.title: If one wants to be contrarian in IT after the recent sell-off, the key point is that the market is currently not differentiating much. Correction has ...
items.description_html:
If one wants to be contrarian in IT after the recent sell-off, the key point is that the market is currently not differentiating much. Correction has been across the board.

Some correction was also necessary. It forces companies to realise that the old way of operating may not work anymore. They need to evolve faster, especially with AI.

If someone wants to take IT exposure purely as a contrarian bet, I would still prefer midcap IT over largecap IT.

In Midcap IT, there are a few better choices like -
Persistent Systems, Coforge, LTIMindtree, Mphasis

Among these, considering execution track record, management commentary & future outlook : Persistent Systems & Coforge seems to be better placed.

Their concalls were also worth listening to, as management shared a lot of useful insights on demand, deal wins, AI adoption, vertical wise growth & future outlook.

The interesting part is that price action in IT stocks is now making many of them look more like yield plays than growth stocks, especially in largecap IT.

With valuations correcting & FCF yields moving closer to 7-8%, markets are almost treating them like bonds.

Now coming to ITBEES -

ITBEES can be a decent way to take broad exposure to the IT sector, but it comes with one limitation.

It is heavily tilted towards largecap IT names.

So if the view is simply that the entire IT sector is oversold & should see mean reversion, then ITBEES can work.

But if the view is that midcap IT will continue outperforming largecap IT, then ITBEES may not be the best way to express that view.

Because ITBEES will mainly give exposure to Infosys, TCS, HCL Tech, Tech Mahindra & Wipro, with limited weight to midcap names.

What are your thoughts, mention below⏬


Equity Insights Elite: CC Growth Trend of IT Companies

Coforge & Persistent Systems continue to be outperformers

Midcap >> Largecap.

RT DD India
India condemns attack on Indian-flagged ship off Oman coast

The Ministry of External Affairs has strongly deplored the attack on an Indian-flagged commercial vessel off the coast of Oman yesterday.

All Indian crew members are safe and have been rescued by Omani authorities.

India has reiterated that targeting commercial shipping and endangering innocent civilian mariners is unacceptable and must stop.

items.title: RT Sann: His name was Rajan. He was a final year engineering student at the Regional Engineering College in Calicut, Kerala. His father, T V Eachara V...
items.description_html:
RT Sann
His name was Rajan.

He was a final year engineering student at the Regional Engineering College in Calicut, Kerala. His father, T V Eachara Varier, was a Hindi professor at the Government Arts and Science College in the same city.

On the morning of March 1 1976 the police came to the college campus and took Rajan away.

India was under Emergency. Civil liberties were suspended. Courts had effectively turned away.

His father found out the next day from the college principal.

He went to every police station in the district. No one admitted to having his son.

He met the Home Minister of Kerala, K Karunakaran, directly.

He sent petitions to the Home Secretary of the Government of Kerala three times. Not a single reply or acknowledgement came.

He wrote to the President of India and the Home Minister of the central government, with copies to every Member of Parliament from Kerala.

Nothing.

What Eachara Varier did not know at the time was that his son had been taken to an illegal police interrogation camp at Kakkayam.

He was tortured.

A practice called uruttal was used, where a heavy wooden log is rolled over the body of the victim.

Rajan died from his injuries. His body was disposed of by the police and was never found.

When the Emergency ended in 1977 Eachara Varier filed a habeas corpus petition in the Kerala High Court.

It was the first such petition filed in Kerala after the Emergency.

He did it without legal training, without political backing, without money.

He had spent everything searching for his son.

The court case slowly unravelled the truth.

It forced K Karunakaran to resign as Chief Minister of Kerala in 1978 when the adverse judgment came.

Rajan’s mother became mentally unstable from the grief. She died in 2000 still not knowing where her son was.

Eachara Varier wrote his memoir, Memories of a Father, which won the Kerala Sahitya Akademi Award in 2004.

In its final lines he wrote, “I don’t close the door. Let the rain lash inside and drench me. Let at least my invisible son know that his father never shut the door.”

He died on April 13 2006. He never found his son’s body.

Rajan was picked up from his college campus on a March morning in 1976. He was never seen again.

Repost this. Some stories must not be allowed to disappear.

items.title: Happy for everyone who made money here in MTAR But fundamentally, valuations are now off the charts In the short term, valuations doesn't matter much ...
items.description_html:
Happy for everyone who made money here in MTAR

But fundamentally, valuations are now off the charts

In the short term, valuations doesn't matter much when momentum is strong

But over the long term, markets do take note of valuations

We have already seen multiple eg where expensive stocks kept moving for some time, but once growth expectations cooled off, the correction was brutal

At high valuations, even a small disappointment in numbers, margins or guidance can lead to sharp derating

Trailing such positions is the better approach

Otherwise, history has shown that the end result in highly expensive stocks is usually not good when expectations become unrealistic


Equity Insights Elite: Lot of discussion around MTAR

Stock is already up 300%

Results are good, but expectations were much higher

Everything aside, current MCap is 20k Cr

This year ended with slightly less than 100 Cr PAT

Even if it does 400-500 Cr PAT, it would still trade at 40-50x

India’s fertiliser sector is entering FY27 amid rising geopolitical uncertainty, elevated input costs, and increasing import dependence.

The ongoing West Asia crisis has triggered sharp spikes in natural gas, ammonia, urea, and DAP prices - raising concerns around fertiliser availability, subsidy burden, and sector profitability ahead of the Kharif season.

Our latest CareEdge Ratings report analyses:
• Impact of the West Asia crisis on fertiliser supply chains
• Rising import dependence in Urea & DAP
• Surge in raw material and natural gas prices
• Implications for fertiliser subsidy budget in FY27
• Outlook for fertiliser companies and sector credit profile

A must-read for stakeholders tracking the Indian fertiliser sector, agri economy, subsidy policy, commodity prices, energy markets, and supply chain risks.

items.title: RT Himanta Biswa Sarma: The Ironman is widely regarded as one of the toughest endurance events in the world. It is a grueling single-day test of stami...
items.description_html:
RT Himanta Biswa Sarma
The Ironman is widely regarded as one of the toughest endurance events in the world. It is a grueling single-day test of stamina and determination. Participants must complete a 3.8 km swim, a 180 km cycling ride, and a full 42.2 km marathon, all back to back.

On 10 May, my son Nandil, currently in his fifth year at the National Law School of India University in Bengaluru, took on this extraordinary challenge at Ironman Vietnam. After just 7-8 months of preparation, he completed the challenge in approximately 16 hours.

As a father, it fills me with immense pride to see his discipline, determination and perseverance. I wish him the very best for the journey ahead and for many more milestones in life.

items.description_html:
RT Chandra R. Srikanth
HCLTech leading Sarvam round is a rare instance of an Indian IT company leading an investment in an AI startup. It also comes at a time when IT companies are struggling to grow.

While HCLTech will invest around $150 million in Sarvam AI, Bessemer Venture Partners (BVP) will put in another $50 million as part of the deal,"

“The remaining $100 million will come jointly from Nvidia, Prosperity7, Activate, Glade Brook and others, including existing backers.

Sarvam AI is backed by investors such as Khosla Ventures, Lightspeed, Peak XV Partners and others which are all expected to participate in the round, per sources

Moneycontrol had exclusively reported on March 24 Sarvam AI had begun talks to raise $250 million from HCLTech, NVIDIA, Accel and others in a fresh funding round which will be among the largest for an Indian AI startup in recent years

A few days later, Moneycontrol was also first to report on April 1 that Accel had passed and Bessemer Venture Partners was actively engaging with the company to close a funding round.

items.title: RT Neelkanth Mishra: With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil p...
items.description_html:
RT Neelkanth Mishra
With China's significant inventory releases calming physical oil markets (physical premiums are down) but pushing up future oil prices (Mar-2027 delivery now reaching $85-90), the economy now needs to adjust to sustained higher oil prices.
Of the 2% of GDP terms-of-trade shock, we estimate the government has absorbed around 1.2% so far, and 0.8% has flowed through. As fuel prices at the pump increase, more of the growth headwinds will show up. But the binding constraint is on the external account (the INR), and the economy needs to adjust to energy market realities.
My column today in the Times of India
https://tessellatum.in/?p=619

items.title: RT Hiba Nasr: ‼️Vance in response to @JenniferJJacobs on the state of the negotiations with Iran: I spent a good amount of time on the phone with wi...
items.description_html:
RT Hiba Nasr
‼️Vance in response to @JenniferJJacobs on the state of the negotiations with Iran:

I spent a good amount of time on the phone with with both Jared Kushner and Steve would call this morning a number of our friends. In the Arab world this morning are making progress. The fundamental question is, do we make enough progress that we satisfy the President's red line? And the red line is very simple. We need to feel confident that we put a number of protections in place such that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon. That is the question, do we meet that threshold or not? I think that we've made a lot of progress since we left Pakistan. I thought we made some progress in Pakistan, but we've made more since then. But that is, fundamentally what we're negotiating towards, is we want to be able to look the American people in the eye and say with confidence that you are not going to have to worry about this very, very dangerous regime having access to the most dangerous weapons of the world that's the goal for focus on, again, there's a lot of ways to accomplish that goal, the President has set us off on the diplomatic pathway for now and that's what I'm focused on.

Indians' love for bling has long been a thorn in the side of policy makers tasked with currency and fiscal management.

In crisis, all we seem to have are temporary fixes like duty hikes and flawed Sovereign Gold Bond schemes that have become an unhedged liability for the government.

items.title: RT Intrinsic Compounding: In FY2004, India’s GDP growth quietly crossed 8%. Most investors missed the shift — because they were still studying the p...
items.description_html:
RT Intrinsic Compounding
In FY2004, India’s GDP growth quietly crossed 8%.
Most investors missed the shift — because they were still studying the previous cycle.

This earnings season, something similar may be happening again.

When you stop focusing on headline “beats” and instead study where the real compounding is happening, a different picture emerges.

Some sectors are posting numbers that would’ve dominated headlines 2 years ago.
Today? They are barely discussed.

That’s exactly what we’re decoding this Sunday.

➯ Topic: Earnings Decoded | Fastest Growing Companies in Q4FY26
➯ Date & Time: 17 May 2026, 1 p.m. (IST)
➯ Registration Link: https://learn.soic.in/learn/fast-checkout/270355?priceId=268752
(Use a browser to enrol)
➯ Venue: Zoom Online
☞ Enrolment for 365 days.
☞ Recording available 48-72 hours after the live session.

What we’ll cover:
▪️Sector-wise earnings heatmap (growth weighted, not noise weighted)
▪️What con-calls are quietly revealing
▪️Key insights Ishmohit has distilled this earnings season
▪️Companies showing explosive growth — and whether it’s sustainable

The puck may already be moving.
The question is: are you watching where it’s going… or where it was?

See you this Sunday at 1 p.m.!

items.title: A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨 Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications: • Non-curr...
items.description_html:
A Masterclass on Cash Flow Statements🚨

Recently reviewed an SME company’s Cash Flow Statement and found some shocking classifications:

• Non-current liabilities adjusted under working capital changes- even though they belong to Financing Activity/(else depend on line item but not shown as part of Operating Activity)

• Current investments deducted from Operating Cash Flow: turning positive CFO into negative -
Either shown as a part of Investing Activity or as a part of Cash Equivalent

• Only gain/loss on sale of assets shown, but actual sale proceeds missing from Investing Activity

• For the first time ever found interesting line item in Cash Flow from Financing Activity - “Proceeds from issue of Security Premium” 👀(Just Disclosure Change - Proceeds from issue of equity share)

Interestingly How this Man matched the Cash Balance?
Comment below - what can be the possible balancing figure here?

One more Interesting part?
The company raised funds to purchase a corporate office. This is exactly why smart investors don’t stop at Revenue, EBITDA or PAT.

The real story hides inside:
• Cash flow classifications
• Notes to accounts
• Working capital adjustments

#redflags

items.title: WABAG and PEAK announce first Bio-CNG plant in Ghaziabad; commercial operations expected within one year This Project marks the first milestone under ...
items.description_html:
WABAG and PEAK announce first Bio-CNG plant in Ghaziabad; commercial operations expected within one year

This Project marks the first milestone under their waste-to-energy initiative announced on January 29, 2024, with a shared vision to establish 100 Bio-CNG plants at sewage treatment facilities across India and other markets.

The Bio-CNG Plant will be developed at the 70 MLD Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) in Dundaheda, Ghaziabad, under a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) model on a Public-Private Partnership basis

The Plant will capture raw biogas generated at the STP, preventing methane emissions into the atmosphere, and upgrade it into high-quality renewable Bio-CNG fuel

WABAG will lead the project execution and undertake operations and maintenance for 15 years.

Good to see WABAG focus on other forms of energy production as well, not just from WATER

Shipbuilding revenue follows an S-curve. And every stage of that curve has a different set of winners.

1) Design - Steel & plates (SAIL, JSW)
2) Block/Hull fabrication - Forgings, castings (Ramkrishna 3) Forgings, Bharat Forge, krishana Defence)

5) Outfitting - Electronics, navigation, sensors (Astra, MTAR, Data Patterns, etc. )

NGC orders expected soon for GRSE and revenue kicks in H2 FY28. The real money in FY29+.

Snippet from GRSE concall

items.title: RT Bhargavi: India is desperate for stable foreign capital. INR is in a virtual free fall. Yet, there is an *absurd* bottleneck nobody talks about: th...
items.description_html:
RT Bhargavi
India is desperate for stable foreign capital. INR is in a virtual free fall. Yet, there is an *absurd* bottleneck nobody talks about: the RBI’s FIRMS portal — mandatory for FC-GPR filings and therefore for receiving FDI share capital — has been down for weeks.

As a result, inward remittances for legitimate equity investments into Indian companies are getting stuck because founders literally cannot complete regulatory filings.

At a moment when the country needs every dollar of productive FDI it can attract, this is exactly the kind of invisible bureaucratic friction India should not be imposing on itself.

@RBI @RBIsays @NITIAyog @nsitharaman

items.title: RT Persistence Capital: Manufacturing will be a larger part of benchmarks going forward as it's one of the few pockets of the market where growth is b...
items.description_html:
RT Persistence Capital
Manufacturing will be a larger part of benchmarks going forward as it's one of the few pockets of the market where growth is broad-based and secular.

As an example, TCS grew revenues by INR 41,563 cr during FY23-26. During that same period, Dixon grew revenues by INR 36,681 cr. Think about it - Dixon's absolute revenue accretion in the last 3 years was ~88% of the country's largest IT services business even though it's FY23 revenue was ~5% of TCS FY23 revenue.

Nifty 50 has negligible exposure to manufacturing businesses. We doubt that this will still be the case a decade from now.

Disclaimer: No recommendation to buy or sell anything.

items.title: Eternal says their Moat is physical - "Every order on Zomato is a real-world coordination problem. A meal has to be prepared by a restaurant we don't ...
items.description_html:
Eternal says their Moat is physical -

"Every order on Zomato is a real-world coordination problem. A meal has to be prepared by a restaurant we don't control, picked up within minutes by a delivery partner navigating traffic we can't predict, and delivered while the food is still hot. Get any one of those wrong, and the experience breaks.

Blinkit takes this complexity several notches up. We run our own supply chain. 17 million square feet of warehousing and dark store space. Procuring, storing, and managing inventory across thousands of stores, then delivering in minutes. The margin for error is measured in seconds and centimetres of shelf space. District operates in a world where tens of thousands of people converge on a single venue for a few hours, and the experience depends on everything from sound engineering and crowd flows to parking, security, and weather.

But here's the thing about the physical world: it doesn't always cooperate. The weather shuts down roads. Demand spikes without warning. Restaurants run out of ingredients mid-rush. Delivery partners get stuck. And if that complexity weren't enough, we build in India. Our systems interact with the real India - cities where addresses are approximate, road infrastructure is always catching up, supply chains that still run largely on cash and handshake relationships, a largely informal workforce, and a merchant ecosystem that is fragmented in ways that would be unrecognizable to most global tech companies.

This isn't a complaint - it's a description of our moat. Because anyone who figures out how to build reliably in this environment has built something very hard to replicate. Over the last two decades, that's what we've done. We've built systems that don't just work in ideal conditions but hold up under real-world stress.

As a result of this reliability, our platforms have become a part of daily life for over 100 million customers in the country. This trust extends across the ecosystem. Today, our platforms support livelihoods for over 1 million delivery partners, 400k+ restaurants, 100k+ supply chain workers, and thousands of brands. As our scale grows, so does the economic value and impact that we create for our partners."

Src - Deepinder Goyal's Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter, no reco

items.title: RT Imtiaz Mahmood: I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We ...
items.description_html:
RT Imtiaz Mahmood
I was on a train in Tokyo. We stopped between stations. Announcement in Japanese, then in English: "We apologize for the delay. We will resume shortly."

The delay was maybe 3 minutes. Not a big deal.

When the train started moving again, another announcement: "We sincerely apologize for the delay. We were stopped for 3 minutes and 20 seconds. This is unacceptable. Thank you for your patience."

Three minutes and twenty seconds. They measured it exactly. And called it unacceptable.

When I got off at my stop, there were station staff on the platform bowing and handing out delay certificates.

I took one out of curiosity. It was an official document stating that the train had been delayed by 3 minutes and 20 seconds, signed and stamped.

The staff member said in English "for your employer. So they know the delay was not your fault."

I said I'm a tourist, I don't need it. He looked confused. "But the delay affected you. You deserve an apology."

Three minutes. They were treating a three-minute delay like a major incident.

Later I mentioned this to a Japanese friend. They said "oh yes, delay certificates are normal. Trains are supposed to be exactly on time. If they are late, they must apologize."

I said three minutes isn't late, it's nothing. My friend said "in Japan, three minutes is late. On time means on time. Not approximately on time."

They said the train company probably investigated why there was a 3-minute delay. "They will find the cause and fix it so it doesn't happen again."

I kept the certificate. It's framed in my apartment now. A reminder that somewhere in the world, people care about three minutes.

© 6IX.

@BSAT_Properties

items.title: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solut...
items.description_html: A lot of defense to IT SIs right now is based on assumption that enterprise clients will not open their systems, data & processes to external AI solutions (Claude, OpenAi). However, this defense collapses the moment we have a precedence from a big enterprise opening up just the way enterprises opened up to cloud after a decade long friction. The moment this starts happening, the JVs OpenAi & Claude have formed will make a killing. And, what will take this wall down? For e.g., enterprises are currently very upset after SAP API policy that prohibited clients from using their own data & sending it to external tools via APIs to architect a solution outside of SAP. This can be the 1st hammer in the wall. One wrong step & clients will find ways to disconnect with enterprise OEMs & will be willing to trust(with an agreement perhaps) likes of Claude & OpenAi. The moment this happens, it is done!! This is the biggest risk I foresee.

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the succe...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the success rate is 6X of national average. A similar thing happened in 2024 too, but the allegations were brushed under the carpet. Had we dealt with it in 2024 with an iron hand, this wouldnt have repeated.

What we know is that 140 of the 180 questions in NEET were part of a guess paper of 410 questions. What is alarming is that the order of the questions and the options were exactly the same.

The modus operandi is simple. In Sikar, the students are called in for a Mock test, a day before the actual exam and made to prepare for each of the questions in the guess paper. The students had 140 of the 180 questions preepared, thus guaranteed 600 of the 720 marks even before they entered the exam hall.

Time to go after the paper leakers. Jail them. 23 Lac students and families will pay the price of the brushing under the carpet we did in 2024. @careers360

items.title: Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter – In 2008, I was collecting restaurant menus on a motorbike in Delhi, scanning them, and uploading ...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter –

In 2008, I was collecting restaurant menus on a motorbike in Delhi, scanning them, and uploading them to a website. I wasn't building a business. I was merely solving a problem I knew others had, in the spirit of service. I never intended it to become a business.

Zomato, the company, was started two years later in 2010, when I started getting advertiser interest on the website, which used to get a little over 5,000 visitors a day.

Over the years, one thing led to another. Last year (FY26), 109 million Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato. The company I accidentally started now touches how India eats, shops, and goes out. But it took us 18 years to get to $10 billion (per year).

This doubling to $20 billion annual NOV will take less than two years from here

Similarly, it took 16 years to reach Adjusted EBITDA profitability in FY24. From there, we expect to reach $1 billion of Adjusted EBITDA, hopefully by FY29

No reco

items.title: RT Rajeev Mantri: 1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces ...
items.description_html:
RT Rajeev Mantri
1/ Owing to high barriers to entry, India’s lodging and hotels industry has become a cartel that raises prices at will and fleeces customers.

India’s luxury hotel average daily rate (ADR) in Mumbai and Delhi now rival those of New York and London. Absurdly enough, the financial press sometimes celebrates this as some triumph of Indian hospitality. It is nothing of the sort. High prices are the unmistakable stamp of a supply-constrained market in a low income country, which should actually have a very competitively priced and deep pool of lodging options. The hotel industry is a structural failure being hyped as a success.

items.title: Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth. Just under 40 million youn...
items.description_html:
Higher education is seen as a key to getting young Indians into skilled, high-paying jobs and accelerating economic growth.

Just under 40 million young people (aged 18-23) were enrolled in higher education programmes in India as of 2022. This included both undergraduate (or equivalent) and postgraduate (or equivalent) levels of study.

However, at 32%, enrolment in tertiary or higher education in India is still below the world average.

In the early 1990s, India’s higher education enrolment rates were similar to China’s, for instance. But in the two decades since, China has made big strides: more than seven in ten young Chinese adults are now in higher education, compared to three in ten young Indian adults.

How does higher education enrolment look across states, and what disciplines do students choose to study in India? Read @akwaghmare’s piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/higher-education/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#College #Undergraduate #Education #HigherEducation #India #DataForIndia

items.title: Arfin India Ltd | Q4 FY26 & FY26 Result Highlights Arfin India reported good Q4 FY26 performance, led by better profitability and sharp margin expansi...
items.description_html:
Arfin India Ltd | Q4 FY26 & FY26 Result Highlights

Arfin India reported good Q4 FY26 performance, led by better profitability and sharp margin expansion.

Q4 FY26 Performance
• Revenue from Operations: ₹19,323.76 lakhs
↳ Up 2.81% QoQ and 26.00% YoY

• EBITDA: ₹1,632.90 lakhs
↳ Up 17.58% QoQ and 147.98% YoY

• EBITDA Margin: 8.45%
↳ Improved by 106 bps QoQ and 416 bps YoY

• PAT: ₹682.43 lakhs
↳ Up 34.04% QoQ and 1,180.60% YoY

FY26 vs FY25
• Revenue: ₹61,799.18 lakhs vs ₹61,575.38 lakhs — 0.36% growth
• EBITDA: ₹4,616.13 lakhs vs ₹3,630.01 lakhs — 27.17% growth
• EBITDA Margin: 7.47% vs 5.90% — up 157 bps
• PAT: ₹1,545.13 lakhs vs ₹914.63 lakhs — 68.93% growth

✅ Key takeaway:
Revenue remained largely stable on a full-year basis, but EBITDA and PAT grew, showing better operating efficiency, improved margins and stronger earnings.

items.title: RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA: This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take. No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare. The UK has...
items.description_html:
RT Anthony DiGiorgio, DO, MHA
This is the ultimate midwit healthcare take.

No, 32 countries have not “figured out” universal healthcare.

The UK has “free” healthcare, and roughly 1 in 3 cancer patients in England still fail to start treatment within 62 days of urgent referral.

Canada has “free” healthcare, and the median wait for neurosurgical treatment is around a year.

Australia has “free” healthcare, and over half the country still buys private insurance despite paying for a public universal system with their taxes.

Switzerland has universal coverage, because residents are required to buy private insurance. There is no government system where benevolent bureaucrats tuck you in at night with a warm blanket and an MRI appointment.

The actual lesson from other wealthy countries is not “they figured it out.”

America’s system has huge problems. Our prices are insane, insurance markets are distorted, and hospital systems are cartelized. Our regulations make care more expensive than it needs to be.

Yet we still guarantee access to even the 8% who don’t have coverage. We give easy routes to qualify for medicaid for those with disabilities.

Pretending the rest of the world solved healthcare because they slapped the word “universal” on a rationing scheme is not analysis.

It is bumper sticker policy for people who think access means having a card in your wallet while you wait a year to see the doctor you need.


daz: Free Universal Healthcare is so complicated and expensive that only 32 of the 33 wealthiest countries in the world have figured it out.
